ori.io.description: Standard Input/Output utilities. Provides functions to read from and write to the terminal console.

ori.fs.description: File system access and management module. Provides synchronous and asynchronous options to check, read, write, and manipulate files and directories on the local machine.

ori.bytes.description: Raw byte buffers utilities. Allows operations such as hex conversions, slice manipulation, and list conversions.

ori.string.description: String utility operations. Provides length checking, splitting, search filters, case modifications, and conversions to raw byte arrays.

ori.math.description: General mathematical constants and helper routines. Contains basic arithmetic bounds checking, rounding, trigonometry, and logarithms.

ori.net.description: Networking module. Provides interfaces for launching TCP/UDP servers and establishing network connection streams.

ori.time.description: Time measurement, clock manipulation, and thread execution suspension routines.

ori.json.description: JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) parser and encoder. Allows serializing Ori values into strings and deserializing JSON payloads back to language structures.

ori.random.description: Pseudorandom number generation (PRNG) routines for generating floats and integers within ranges.

ori.path.description: File system path parser. Allows joining segments, resolving absolute paths, and extracting filenames or extensions.

ori.list.description: List manipulation core module. Houses size checks, indexing operations, pushing, popping, and slice manipulations.

ori.map.description: Hash map (key-value dictionary) utilities for storing, retrieving, and checking keys.

ori.set.description: Unique set collection helpers for set arithmetic, values membership tests, inserts, and removes.

ori.io.print.description: Writes a string to the standard output without appending a newline.
ori.io.print.applicability: Used for printing prompt messages, building progress indicators, or custom formatted output.
ori.io.print.example:
import ori.io as io

func main()
    io.print("Enter value: ")
end

ori.io.println.description: Writes a string to the standard output, followed by a newline.
ori.io.println.applicability: Used for printing log messages, general command line outputs, and standard debugging text.
ori.io.println.example:
import ori.io as io

func main()
    io.println("Application started successfully.")
end

ori.fs.read_file.description: Reads the entire contents of a file into a bytes buffer asynchronously/synchronously depending on execution context.
ori.fs.read_file.applicability: Essential for processing binary files, loading text resources, or parsing local configuration files.
ori.fs.read_file.example:
import ori.fs as fs
import ori.io as io

func main()
    const res = fs.read_file("config.json")
    match res
        success(data) => io.println("Loaded data: " + string(data))
        error(err) => io.println("Failed to read config.")
    end
end

ori.fs.write_file.description: Writes a bytes buffer into a file, creating it if it does not exist, or overwriting it if it does.
ori.fs.write_file.applicability: Used for saving application state, writing logs, creating temporary storage, or exporting reports.
ori.fs.write_file.example:
import ori.fs as fs
import ori.io as io

func main()
    const payload = bytes.from_list([72, 101, 108, 108, 111]) // "Hello"
    const res = fs.write_file("output.txt", payload)
    match res
        success(void) => io.println("Write complete.")
        error(err) => io.println("Write failed.")
    end
end

ori.bytes.to_hex.description: Converts a raw bytes buffer into its hexadecimal string representation.
ori.bytes.to_hex.applicability: Widely used in cryptographic hashing visualizations, generating checksum strings, and formatting binary protocol payloads for debugging.

ori.bytes.from_hex.description: Parses a hexadecimal string and returns the corresponding raw bytes buffer.
ori.bytes.from_hex.applicability: Useful for decoding hex-encoded files, processing public-key certificates, or parsing network packets represented as hex.
ori.bytes.from_hex.example:
import ori.bytes as bytes
import ori.io as io

func main()
    const hex_str = "48656c6c6f"
    const data = bytes.from_hex(hex_str)
    io.println(string(data)) // Prints "Hello"
end

ori.math.utils.clamp_int.description: Restricts an integer value to be within a specified minimum and maximum range.
ori.math.utils.clamp_int.applicability: Commonly used in game logic (constraining characters within screen boundaries), UI layouts (clamping coordinate systems), and index math.

ori.math.utils.lerp.description: Performs linear interpolation between two float values based on a weight ratio.
ori.math.utils.lerp.applicability: Essential for graphics development, game engines (smooth translation/transitions), and easing animations.

ori.string.utils.contains.description: Checks if a string contains a specific substring.
ori.string.utils.contains.applicability: Used in text processing, simple search filters, and matching query parameters.

ori.random.int_range.description: Generates a pseudorandom integer between the specified min and max bounds (inclusive of min, exclusive of max).
ori.random.int_range.applicability: Commonly used in gaming (rolling dice, picking indexes), generating unique IDs, and random selection algorithms.

ori.random.float.description: Generates a pseudorandom floating-point number between 0.0 and 1.0.
ori.random.float.applicability: Useful for generating percentages, probability weights, random noise multipliers, and easing variations.

ori.time.sleep.description: Suspends execution of the current thread/task for a given duration in milliseconds.
ori.time.sleep.applicability: Used in loops to set intervals, rate-limiting requests, implementing game cycles, or delays in automation.
ori.time.sleep.example:
import ori.time as time
import ori.io as io

func main()
    io.println("Waiting...")
    time.sleep(1000) // Delay 1 second
    io.println("Finished.")
end

ori.time.now.description: Returns the current system timestamp in UNIX epoch seconds or milliseconds.
ori.time.now.applicability: Used for benchmarks, logging date metrics, calculating task durations, and generating unique ID seeds.

ori.json.encode.description: Serializes supported Ori data structures (like maps, lists, and structs) into a JSON formatted string.
ori.json.encode.applicability: Used for exporting application configs, communicating with REST APIs, and storing structured logs.

ori.json.decode.description: Deserializes a JSON string payload back into dynamic Ori objects or types.
ori.json.decode.applicability: Required for parsing API responses, loading configuration files, or handling JSON log structures.
ori.json.decode.example:
import ori.json as json
import ori.io as io

func main()
    const payload = "{\"active\":true}"
    const config = json.decode(payload)
    match config
        success(obj) => io.println("Decoded successfully")
        error(err) => io.println("Failed to decode JSON")
    end
end

ori.path.join.description: Combines list segments into a unified file system path, automatically resolving separator slashes.
ori.path.join.applicability: Used to dynamically build local paths cross-platform without worrying about '/' vs '\' differences.

ori.string.split.description: Splits a string by a delimiter substring, returning a list of segments.
ori.string.split.applicability: Useful for CSV processing, parsing command arguments, and text segment extraction.

float.description: Built-in float conversion primitive. Converts an integer to its floating-point representation.

int.description: Built-in integer identity/conversion primitive. Returns the integer value unchanged.

len.description: Built-in length primitive. Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.

string.description: Built-in string conversion primitive. Converts a value to its string representation.

ori.description: Core language runtime module. Provides fundamental runtime operations.

ori.Error.description: Core error type for the Ori runtime. Represents structured error values with code and message.

ori.atomic.description: Atomic operations for lock-free concurrent value access. Provides atomic load, store, and add operations.

ori.bytes.algorithms.description: Byte buffer comparison and prefix-checking algorithms.

ori.bytes.utils.description: Byte buffer utility helpers. Provides convenience wrappers for common byte operations.

ori.channel.description: Channel-based message passing for concurrent communication between tasks.

ori.concurrent.description: Concurrency primitives for shared-state coordination between tasks.

ori.concurrent.utils.description: Concurrency utility helpers. Provides typed copy and transfer operations for concurrent-safe values.

ori.convert.description: Type conversion primitives between basic types (bool, float, string, int).

ori.convert.utils.description: Safe type conversion utilities with fallback default values.

ori.core.description: Core runtime internals. Low-level primitives used by the language runtime.

ori.deque.description: Double-ended queue (deque) data structure. Supports efficient insertion and removal from both ends.

ori.deque.utils.description: Deque utility helpers with typed convenience wrappers for int-typed deques.

ori.doubly_linked_list.description: Doubly-linked list data structure. Supports bidirectional traversal, cursor-based navigation, and O(1) insertion/removal at known positions.

ori.doubly_linked_list.utils.description: Doubly-linked list utility helpers with typed wrappers for int-typed lists.

ori.files.description: Low-level file descriptor operations. Internal module for file handle management.

ori.format.description: Value formatting primitives for rendering numbers, dates, bytes, and other types as human-readable strings.

ori.format.utils.description: Format utility helpers. Provides additional formatting functions with custom parameters.

ori.fs.utils.description: File system utility helpers. Provides convenience wrappers with fallback defaults for common file operations.

ori.hash_table.utils.description: Hash table utility helpers with typed convenience wrappers for string-keyed integer-value maps.

ori.heap.utils.description: Heap utility helpers with typed convenience wrappers for int-typed heaps.

ori.io.utils.description: I/O utility helpers. Provides convenience wrappers for printing and reading with fallback defaults.

ori.iter.description: Iterator functional operations. Provides map, filter, reduce, sort, and other higher-order functions for sequences.

ori.iter.utils.description: Iterator utility helpers. Provides convenience functions for common iterator patterns on int sequences.

ori.json.utils.description: JSON utility helpers. Provides convenience wrappers for reading and writing JSON files.

ori.lazy.description: Lazy evaluation primitives. Defers computation until the value is first accessed.

ori.linked_list.description: Singly-linked list data structure. Supports O(1) insertion at head, cursor-based traversal, and sequential access patterns.

ori.linked_list.utils.description: Linked list utility helpers with typed wrappers for int-typed lists.

ori.list.algorithms.description: List algorithms. Provides specialized operations like binary search, equality checks, and aggregation on int-typed lists.

ori.list.utils.description: List utility helpers. Provides convenience accessors with fallback defaults.

ori.map.algorithms.description: Map algorithms. Provides batch operations like merging, inversion, key counting, and value aggregation on typed maps.

ori.map.utils.description: Map utility helpers. Provides convenience accessors with fallback defaults for map lookups.

ori.math.algorithms.description: Mathematical algorithms. Provides advanced numeric operations like clamping, interpolation, and approximation.

ori.math.utils.description: Math utility helpers. Provides additional numeric functions with type-specific variants.

ori.mem.description: Memory management primitives. Provides low-level allocation and deallocation operations.

ori.net.utils.description: Network utility helpers. Provides convenience wrappers for connection management with fallback defaults.

ori.os.description: Operating system interaction primitives. Provides access to environment variables, process info, platform detection, and directory navigation.

ori.os.utils.description: OS utility helpers. Provides convenience wrappers with fallback defaults for platform and environment queries.

ori.process.utils.description: Process utility helpers. Provides convenience accessors for process execution results.

ori.queue.utils.description: Queue utility helpers with typed convenience wrappers for int and string queues.

ori.random.utils.description: Random utility helpers. Provides additional random generation functions including seeded generators and collection shuffling.

ori.set.algorithms.description: Set algorithms. Provides batch operations like union, intersection, difference, and disjoint checks on typed sets.

ori.set.utils.description: Set utility helpers. Provides convenience functions for set membership testing and construction.

ori.stack.utils.description: Stack utility helpers with typed convenience wrappers for int and string stacks.

ori.string.algorithms.description: String algorithms. Provides comparison, joining, repetition, and truncation operations.

ori.string.utils.description: String utility helpers. Provides additional string operations for case conversion, counting, padding, and text analysis.

ori.task.description: Async task management. Provides spawning, joining, cancellation, and coordination of asynchronous computations.

ori.test.description: Test assertion primitives. Provides functions for comparing values, checking allocations, and managing test execution.

ori.test.utils.description: Test utility helpers. Provides convenience assertion wrappers for common equality and boolean checks.

ori.time.utils.description: Time utility helpers. Provides arithmetic operations on durations and convenience functions for time conversion.

ori.tree.description: Tree data structure. Supports hierarchical node management with pre-order, post-order, and breadth-first traversals.

ori.tree.algorithms.description: Tree algorithms. Provides queries for leaf detection, leaf counting, depth calculation, and value collection.

ori.validate.description: Input validation primitives. Provides predicates for checking ranges, emptiness, lengths, and custom conditions.

ori.atomic.new.description: Creates a new empty instance of the atomic collection.

ori.test.collect_cycles.description: Triggers cycle collection and returns the number of cycles collected.

ori.test.fail.description: Immediately fails the current test with a message.

ori.test.live_allocations.description: Returns the number of currently live memory allocations.

ori.test.utils.equal_int.description: Performs the equal int operation on utils.

ori.test.utils.equal_text.description: Performs the equal text operation on utils.

ori.test.utils.is_false.description: Performs the is false operation on utils.

ori.test.utils.is_true.description: Performs the is true operation on utils.

ori.test.utils.not_equal_int.description: Performs the not equal int operation on utils.

ori.test.utils.not_equal_text.description: Performs the not equal text operation on utils.

ori.time.duration_ms.description: Returns the elapsed time in milliseconds since a reference point.

ori.time.utils.add.description: Adds an element to the set. Returns true if the element was newly inserted.

ori.time.utils.diff.description: Performs the diff operation on utils.

ori.time.utils.from_unix_ms.description: Performs the from unix ms operation on utils.

ori.time.utils.hours.description: Performs the hours operation on utils.

ori.time.utils.milliseconds.description: Performs the milliseconds operation on utils.

ori.time.utils.minutes.description: Performs the minutes operation on utils.

ori.time.utils.seconds.description: Performs the seconds operation on utils.

ori.time.utils.since.description: Performs the since operation on utils.

ori.time.utils.sleep_ms.description: Performs the sleep ms operation on utils.

ori.time.utils.sub.description: Performs the sub operation on utils.

ori.time.utils.to_unix_ms.description: Performs the to unix ms operation on utils.

ori.time.utils.until.description: Performs the until operation on utils.

ori.tree.add_child.description: Adds a child node to the specified parent node.

ori.tree.algorithms.is_leaf.description: Performs the is leaf operation on algorithms.

ori.tree.algorithms.leaf_count.description: Performs the leaf count operation on algorithms.

ori.tree.algorithms.max_depth_from.description: Performs the max depth from operation on algorithms.

ori.tree.algorithms.values_preorder.description: Performs the values preorder operation on algorithms.

ori.tree.breadth_first.description: Traverses the tree level by level and returns node values.

ori.tree.children.description: Returns the direct children of the specified node.

ori.tree.clone.description: Returns a deep copy of the collection, independent of the original.

ori.tree.clone_subtree.description: Creates a deep copy of the subtree rooted at the given node.

ori.tree.contains_node.description: Returns true if the tree contains the specified node.

ori.tree.depth.description: Returns the depth of the specified node from the root.

ori.tree.find.description: Searches for a node matching the predicate and returns its value.

ori.tree.len.description: Returns the number of elements in the collection.

ori.tree.move_subtree.description: Moves a subtree to become a child of a new parent node.

ori.tree.new.description: Creates a new empty instance of the tree collection.

ori.tree.parent.description: Returns the parent node of the specified node.

ori.tree.post_order.description: Traverses the tree in post-order (children, root) and returns node values.

ori.tree.pre_order.description: Traverses the tree in pre-order (root, children) and returns node values.

ori.tree.remove_subtree.description: Removes the subtree rooted at the specified node.

ori.tree.root.description: Returns the root node of the tree.

ori.tree.set_value.description: Replaces the value stored at the specified node.

ori.tree.try_value.description: Returns the value at the node, or an error if the node is not found.

ori.tree.value.description: Returns the value stored at the specified node.

ori.validate.all_digits.description: Returns true if the string contains only digit characters.

ori.validate.between.description: Returns true if the value is between the specified min and max (exclusive).

ori.validate.blank.description: Returns true if the string is empty or contains only whitespace.

ori.validate.even.description: Returns true if the integer is even.

ori.validate.in_range.description: Returns true if the value is within the specified range (inclusive).

ori.validate.int_in_range.description: Returns true if the integer is within the specified range (inclusive).

ori.validate.length_between.description: Returns true if the string length is between the specified min and max.

ori.validate.length_equal.description: Returns true if the string length equals the specified value.

ori.validate.matches_length.description: Returns true if the string length matches the specified value.

ori.validate.max_len.description: Returns true if the string has at most the maximum length.

ori.validate.max_length.description: Returns true if the string has at most the maximum length.

ori.validate.min_len.description: Returns true if the string has at least the minimum length.

ori.validate.min_length.description: Returns true if the string has at least the minimum length.

ori.validate.negative.description: Returns true if the number is less than zero.

ori.validate.non_negative.description: Returns true if the number is greater than or equal to zero.

ori.validate.non_zero.description: Returns true if the number is not zero.

ori.validate.not_empty.description: Returns true if the collection is not empty.

ori.validate.not_empty_text.description: Returns true if the string is not empty.

ori.validate.odd.description: Returns true if the integer is odd.

ori.validate.one_of.description: Returns true if the value matches one of the allowed values.

ori.validate.one_of_string.description: Returns true if the string equals one of the allowed values.

ori.validate.positive.description: Returns true if the number is greater than zero.

ori.validate.string_not_blank.description: Returns true if the string is not blank.

ori.graph.description: Graph data structure. Supports directed and weighted graphs with BFS, DFS, shortest path, topological sort, and cycle detection.

ori.graph.algorithms.description: Graph algorithms. Provides path reachability, connectivity checks, and reachability counting.

ori.hash_table.description: Hash table (key-value store) data structure. Provides O(1) average-case insertion, lookup, and deletion of key-value pairs.

ori.heap.description: Binary heap (priority queue) data structure. Supports efficient insertion and extraction of the highest-priority element.

ori.process.description: System process execution. Provides functions to run shell commands and capture their output.

ori.queue.description: FIFO queue data structure. Supports enqueue, dequeue, and peek operations.

ori.stack.description: LIFO stack data structure. Supports push, pop, and peek operations.
